data.title: Cathie Wood Scoops Up $24M in Figma, Sells DraftKings
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) made notable portfolio changes on Thursday, February 19, as shown in daily fund disclosures. Wood bought roughly $24 million worth of Figma (FIG) stock, following its robust Q4FY25 results. Her purchase reflects solid confidence in the newly listed cloud-based collaborative design platform's future prospects. She also acquired shares of British drug maker Compass Pathways (CMPS) and biotech firm Recursion Pharmaceuticals (RXRX).

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

On the other hand, Wood offloaded a large portion of betting platform DraftKings (DKNG) and social media site Pinterest (PINS). Let's briefly review Wood's trades:

Figma – Wood added 964,342 shares, while its share price surged 6.9% yesterday. Figma beat both sales and earnings estimates, and CEO Dylan Field pushed back on fears that generative AI tools could slow the growth of traditional software companies. The company is also leveraging AI to boost its platform. Its Figma Make tool lets users type prompts that AI models from Anthropic and Google (GOOGL) turn into app prototypes. Starting in March, Figma will charge for AI usage through monthly credit limits and pay-per-use pricing.

Compass Pathways – The ARK Genomic Revolution ETF (ARKG) purchased 781,085 shares for $6.5 million, capitalizing on the 2.3% dip in share price. Shares fell after announcing a 17.5 million secondary offering priced at $8 per piece.

Recursion Pharmaceuticals – Wood bought 780,532 RXRX shares for $2.9 million, following a 5.4% surge in its stock price. The surge follows the prior day's decline, when Nvidia (NVDA) disclosed in a 13F filing that it had fully exited its position in the AI-driven drug maker during Q4.  

DraftKings – The ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK) sold 480,919 shares for about $10.8 million, continuing with her steady exit from the gaming platform.

Pinterest – The ARK Next Generation Internet ETF (ARKW) sold 158,945 shares for $2.66 million, aligning with her recent selling spree after it reported lackluster Q4 2025 results and issued a weak outlook.

Wood's Strategic Portfolio Shuffle

Let's see how these stocks perform using the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool.

Currently, analysts have a "Strong Buy" consensus rating on DraftKings and Compass Pathways, with CMPS stock offering the highest upside potential over the next twelve months.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: What's Driving Herbalife Stock (HLF) to a 52-Week High?
data.description:
Herbalife Ltd.'s (HLF) stock climbed to a 52-week high on Thursday after the soccer superstar Cristiano Ronaldo invested $7.5 million for a 10% stake in HBL Pro2col Software LLC, a health and wellness tracking platform owned by Herbalife. The company also reported solid Q4 2025 results that beat analysts' expectations, further fueling optimism around the stock's outlook. HLF stock gained 18.32% on Thursday and rose another 1.12% in after-hours trading, extending its strong momentum.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, Herbalife sells dietary supplements, weight management products, and personal care items through a direct-selling business model. Meanwhile, Pro2col's platform helps users monitor fitness, nutrition, and lifestyle data to support personalized wellness goals.

Cristiano Ronaldo Backs Herbalife

Ronaldo explained that his investment is the next step in his long-standing partnership with Herbalife. Notably, Ronaldo, who remains the highest-paid football player in the world, has been a paid endorser of Herbalife since 2013.

He said deepening his involvement with the company motivates him at this stage of his career and aligns with his focus on health and performance. Ronaldo added that the investment "felt like a natural evolution" and is about helping shape and grow a platform that can change how people engage with their health and wellness.

Mizuho Securities' four-star-rated analyst John Baumgartner called Ronaldo's investment "a nice add" for Herbalife.

Herbalife Reports Q4 Results

Herbalife reported Q4 adjusted earnings per share of $0.45, beating analyst estimates of $0.43. At the same time, the company generated $1.3 billion in revenue, surpassing the consensus forecast of $1.25 billion.

Sales increased 6.3% year-over-year, reflecting steady underlying demand despite foreign exchange headwinds.

Is HLF a Good Stock to Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on HLF stock based on three Buys, one Hold, and one Sell assigned in the past three months. Furthermore, the average Herbalife's stock price target of $15.60 per share implies a downside of 20.3% from current levels.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Nvidia Nears $30B Stake in OpenAI, Ditches $100B Infrastructure Deal
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) is close to investing up to $30 billion in OpenAI, replacing its earlier $100 billion infrastructure commitment, according to The Financial Times. The companies are negotiating a direct stake as part of OpenAI's latest funding round, which carries a $730 billion pre-money valuation. Unlike their September announcement, this investment is not tied to development milestones.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The deal differs from last fall's $100 billion infrastructure partnership for OpenAI's computing needs. Nvidia could join later rounds to fulfill the original plan. The deal is not final and the details could change as discussions proceed. Other investors include Amazon (AMZN), SoftBank (SFTBY), and Microsoft (MSFT).

The companies face criticism for multibillion-dollar deals that create circular financing: OpenAI uses funds from Nvidia to buy Nvidia hardware. Rumors of a rift over the $100 billion deal surfaced earlier this year, but OpenAI CEO Sam Altman and Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang dismissed them.

Why This Matters to Nvidia

The investment aligns with Nvidia's strategy. Its market capitalization reached nearly $5 trillion, propelled by surging AI demand, where OpenAI functions as both a key client and a growth catalyst. OpenAI's advancements, from GPT-4 to the latest o-series models, rely on Nvidia's H100 and upcoming B200 chips.

Nonetheless, intensifying competition from Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), custom silicon initiatives by Google (GOOGL) and Amazon, and nascent AI chip startups demands a protective stake in a leading AI enterprise like OpenAI.

How It Could Shape OpenAI's Roadmap

For OpenAI, this capital infusion would bolster its ambitious technical roadmap, as Altman has noted. Developing frontier models like GPT-5 demands unprecedented computational resources. Positioning Nvidia as both infrastructure partner and significant investor would secure priority access to cutting-edge chips amid shortages and aligns incentives for AI breakthroughs.

It remains to be seen how Microsoft will respond to this deal, whether it triggers regulatory scrutiny, and if it prompts similar vertical integration initiatives by AMD, Intel (INTC), or cloud providers seeking to strengthen their AI positions.

Is NVDA Stock a Buy?

Nvidia commands a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 35 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell rating on TipRanks. The average Nvidia price target of $261.84 implies 39.3% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, NVDA shares have surged 34%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Lucid Stock Forecast Ahead of Earnings. Here Is What to Expect
data.description:
Luxury electric vehicle (EV) maker Lucid Group (LCID) will report its Q4 results on February 24. The stock has lost 8% year-to-date. The company's fourth-quarter deliveries improved, rising more than 70% year-over-year as production of its new Gravity SUV ramped up. However, investors remain cautious ahead of the report due to high costs, strong competition from Tesla (TSLA), and cheaper EV rivals. Also, Lucid still relies on outside funding as it works toward profitability, which continues to weigh on sentiment.

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Lose Momentum on Record-High Goods Deficit; Trump's Warning to Iran
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed Thursday in negative territory as investors digested a trade balance update from the Commerce Department amid rising tensions between the U.S. and Iran.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Over the course of 2025, the average U.S. import tariff rate rose to 13% from 2.6%. However, that did little to improve the U.S. trade deficit, which edged lower by just 0.2% to $901.5 billion from $904 billion in 2024. In addition, the deficit for December was $70 billion, above the consensus estimate of $55.5 billion and rising from $56.8 billion in November.

Furthermore, the goods trade deficit for 2025 reached an all-time high of $1.24 trillion and widened by 2.1% from 2024. The U.S. carried the largest goods deficit with the European Union at $218.8 billion, with China trailing behind at $202.1 billion and Mexico at $196.9 billion. At the same time, the goods deficit with China was the lowest in 21 years.

Meanwhile, President Trump gave Iran a deadline of around 10 days to either reach a deal with the U.S. to halt its nuclear program or face military action. "Good talks are being had. It's proven to be, over the years, not easy to make a meaningful deal with Iran," Trump said at the first Board of Peace meeting in Washington, D.C. "We have to make a meaningful deal, otherwise bad things happen."

In recent days, the U.S. military has increased its military presence in the Middle East as negotiations continue between the two sides.

Elsewhere, data from the Department of Labor shows that employers remain hesitant to both fire and hire workers. For the week ended February 14, initial jobless claims fell by 23,000 to 206,000, below the consensus estimate of 225,000. Initial jobless claims are at their lowest level in five weeks, while the weekly drop was the largest since November. Continuing jobless claims, which trail initial claims by a week, increased by 17,000 to 1.869 million and were above the estimate of 1.86 million.

"Workers have been finding it more difficult to find new jobs, and soft labor demand may prevent the unemployment rate from declining further in the months ahead," said Bloomberg economist Stuart Paul.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.28% loss, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) fell by 0.41%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: 'Time to Bail Out,' Says Investor About Strategy Stock (MSTR)
data.description:
Strategy (NASDAQ:MSTR) stock has been closely tied to bitcoin's price swings, amplifying both the rallies and the drawdowns. When BTC surged, MSTR outperformed; when bitcoin corrected, the stock fell even harder. The result has been extreme volatility, with shares down 59% over the past 12 months.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

That kind of volatility is the direct result of what Strategy has become – essentially a bitcoin accumulation vehicle. With Michael Saylor leading the charge, the playbook is simple – raise capital, buy bitcoin, and steadily increase the amount of BTC owned per fully diluted share. Management even created its own scoreboard, "BTC Yield," which tracks the percentage change in bitcoin per share and gives investors a straightforward way to judge whether the strategy is actually working.

That structure is exactly what initially caught investor Marc Gerstein's attention. He didn't see it as reckless speculation, but as a deliberate way to gain amplified bitcoin exposure through disciplined leverage. As he grew more comfortable with the bitcoin thesis itself, he upgraded the stock to a Buy – especially since the earlier rounds of purchases were funded in a fairly straightforward way, using equity issuance and low-cost convertible debt.

Given the use of leverage, the company readily acknowledged that its stock was bound to show more volatility than bitcoin itself, but it expected equity holders to benefit from enhanced returns over time.

But Gerstein thinks the company no longer operates via that model. The framework shifted in 2025, when the prior intelligent-leverage model evolved into a more complex and aggressive digital credit strategy. The company now funds bitcoin acquisitions in part through the issuance of high-yield preferred stock.

So, what does Gerstein think investors should do with the stock now its strategy has changed? Gerstein concedes that in the past, MSTR has delivered returns that significantly exceeded those of bitcoin. However, as the saying goes, past performance offers no guarantee of future results, and in any case, more recently, the stock has seriously lagged bitcoin.

This raises the question of how to interpret the "rapid transition" from MSTR trading at a substantial premium to the value of its Bitcoin holdings to now trading at a discount.

"There is no simple answer," says Gerstein, although he actually does go on to give a fairly straightforward answer. "I previously believed that MSTR's intelligent leverage was worth a premium," Gerstein noted. "But given the way intelligent leverage gave way to digital credit, etc., I can no longer say that."

The investor believes this raises MSTR's risk profile and volatility to levels that extend beyond "anything contemplated by standard statistical analysis."

"Based on this," Gerstein summed up, "MSTR equity no longer appeals to me, so I now rate it "Sell." (To watch Gerstein's track record, click here)

Gerstein's bearish take differs from the one offered by all Street analysts covering this stock. Based on a unanimous 11 positive reviews, MSTR claims a Strong Buy consensus rating. There are also plenty of gains projected here; at $352.27, the average target suggests shares will surge by 172% over the next 12 months. (See MSTR stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Tesla Stock (NASDAQ:TSLA) Notches Up as Edmunds Tests the Model 3 Against the Model Y
data.description:
Electric vehicle giant Tesla (TSLA) is not exactly known for wallet-friendly vehicles. However, two of Tesla's models are at least feasible for the typical driver, specifically, the Model 3 and the Model Y. But which one comes out ahead in a straight comparison? A recent Edmunds study took this point on, and what it found might surprise you. The news was good enough for investors, though, who gave Tesla shares a fractional boost in Thursday afternoon's trading.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Edmunds recently staged a side-by-side comparison at a test track to see if the Model Y Performance or the Model 3 Performance performed better under key conditions. The Model Y had a bit of an advantage going in, as it offered 510 horsepower as well as some "upgraded hardware" that would make it a better driver. But would the Model 3 be more than the sum of its parts, and end up superior to the Model Y?

The Model Y went from zero to 60 in 3.6 seconds, which was about half a second longer than the Model 3, reports noted. Reversing that trend also gave the advantage to the Model 3, as the Model Y needed 121 feet to go from 60 to zero. The Model 3, meanwhile, needed 115 feet. The Model 3 also beat the Model Y in lateral grip, with the Model Y delivering 0.91 g and the Model 3 bringing in 0.97 g. While the Model Y has improved significantly, the Model 3 seems to deliver better for speed. And those who want an SUV, meanwhile, should avoid the Model Y Performance to focus on the standard Model Y instead.

Your Car May Already Be a Hacker Target

A new study out from Northeastern suggests that your Tesla—specifically the Model 3 or the Cybertruck—are actually hackable. The wireless systems these cars use can be used to not only track vehicle locations, but also have their communications systems disrupted and even "…interfere with network performance."

It is not specifically the Tesla vehicles that are at risk, either; the Northeastern study suggests that "…all modern connected cars" share these risks. They are effectively "computers on wheels," and thus represent the same dangers that a regular computer would face. This is particularly true given that these cars "maintain persistent connectivity to support remote diagnostics, OTA updates and application communications," reports note.

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 16.06% rally in its share price over the past year, the average TSLA price target of $396.80 per share implies 3.17% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Why Are Stock Market Futures Up Today, 2/19/26?
data.description:
U.S. stock market futures moved higher late Thursday night as investors awaited key economic data and a possible Supreme Court decision on President Donald Trump's tariffs. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were up 0.23%, 0.19%, and 0.19%, respectively, at 8:43 p.m. EST on February 19.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Wall Street expects the court may decide whether the tariffs are legal under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act. Traders think stocks could rise if the duties are struck down, even though the White House may later try other ways to reintroduce them.

On the economic front, traders will get fresh data with the release of fourth‑quarter GDP, which economists expect to show 2.5% growth after a stronger‑than‑expected 4.4% gain in Q3. Also, the Fed's preferred inflation gauge, the Personal Consumption Expenditure, is due tomorrow. Estimates call for a 2.8% annual increase in headline PCE and a 3% rise in core PCE.

In the regular trading session, stocks fell, ending a three‑day winning streak for the S&P 500 and the Dow, as oil prices hit a six‑month high amid rising U.S.–Iran tensions. The Dow dropped 0.5%, while the S&P 500 and Nasdaq each slipped 0.3%.

data.title: Why Strategy's (MSTR) Leveraged Bitcoin Play Faces Judgement Day
data.description:
Crypto bull pioneer Strategy (MSTR), formerly known as MicroStrategy, has spent the past several years transforming itself from a quiet enterprise software firm into one of the most aggressive financial vehicles in corporate America. The business still sells analytics tools, but that operation now sits in the shadow of a far larger bet: hundreds of thousands of Bitcoin (BTC-USD) piled onto the balance sheet, financed through waves of convertible debt, preferred shares, and equity issuance.
